name: kipoi-conda

metadata.name: kipoi-conda
metadata.summary: kipoi-conda: conda/pip related functionality used by Kipoi

documentation: kipoi-conda: conda/pip related functionality used by Kipoi

methods.name: kipoi_conda.dependencies.Dependencies.__attrs_post_init__
documentation:
In case conda or pip are filenames pointing to existing files,
read the files and populate the package names

methods.name: kipoi_conda.dependencies.Dependencies.all_installed
documentation:
Validate if all the dependencies are installed as requested

Args:
  verbose: if True, display warnings if the dependencies are not installed

Returns:
  (bool): True if all the required package versions are installed
    and False otherwise

methods.name: kipoi_conda.dependencies.Dependencies.merge
documentation:
Merge one dependencies with another one

Use case: merging the dependencies of model and dataloader

Args:
    dependencies: Dependencies instance

Returns:
    new Dependencies instance

methods.name: kipoi_conda.dependencies.Dependencies.normalized
documentation: Normalize the list of dependencies

methods.name: kipoi_conda.dependencies.Dependencies._get_channels_packages
documentation: Get conda channels and packages separated from each other(by '::')

methods.name: kipoi_conda.dependencies.Dependencies.to_env_file
documentation: Dump the dependencies to a file

methods.name: kipoi_conda.dependencies.Dependencies.gpu
documentation: Get the gpu - version of the dependencies

methods.name: kipoi_conda.dependencies.Dependencies.osx
documentation: Get the os - x compatible dependencies

modules.name: kipoi_conda.utils
documentation:
Conda helper files

Reusing code from: https://github.com/conda/conda-api/blob/master/conda_api.py

functions.name: kipoi_conda.utils.create_env
documentation:
Create new environment given parsed dependencies

Args:
  conda_dependencies: OrderedDict of the `dependencies` field in Conda's environment.yaml.
    `model.dependencies.conda` field in Model or Dataloader.
  env_name: Environment name

functions.name: kipoi_conda.utils.call_script_in_env
documentation:
run an python script in a certain conda enviroment in a background
process.

Args:
    filename (str): path to the python script
    env_name (str or None, optional): Name of the conda enviroment.
    use_current_python (bool, False) If true, the current python executable will be used
    (this is useful for testing purposes )
    args (None, optional): args to pass to the script

Returns:
    Popen: instance of Popen / running program

functions.name: kipoi_conda.utils.get_kipoi_bin
documentation: Returns the path to the kipoi executable in {env_name} environment

functions.name: kipoi_conda.utils.install_conda
documentation:
Install conda packages

Args:
  conda_deps: list of conda packages to be installed
  channels: list of conda channels to use

functions.name: kipoi_conda.utils.get_envs
documentation:
Return all of the (named) environment (this does not include the root
environment), as a list of absolute path to their prefixes.

functions.name: kipoi_conda.utils.parse_conda_package
documentation:
Parse conda package into channel and environment

Args:
  dep: string in the form '<channel>::<package>' or '<package>'

Returns:
  tuple: ('<channel>', '<package>') or ('defaults', '<package>')

functions.name: kipoi_conda.utils.version_split
documentation:
Split the string by the version:
mypacakge<=2.4,==2.4 -> (mypacakge, <=2.4,==2.4)

In [40]: version_split("asdsda>=2.4,==2")
Out[40]: ('asdsda', ['>=2.4', '==2'])

In [41]: version_split("asdsda>=2.4")
Out[41]: ('asdsda', ['>=2.4'])

In [42]: version_split("asdsda")
Out[42]: ('asdsda', [])

functions.name: kipoi_conda.utils.normalize_pip
documentation:
Normalize a list of pip dependencies

Args:
  pip_list: list of pip dependencies

Returns:
  normalized pip

functions.name: kipoi_conda.utils.get_package_version
documentation:
Get installed package version

# Arguments
  package: package name. Example: `kipoi`

# Returns
  (str) if the package is installed and None otherwise

functions.name: kipoi_conda.utils.is_installed
documentation:
Test if the package is installed

# Arguments
  package: package string, can also contain version: mypacakge<=2.4,==2.4
